name: Wolfgang Puck's Lentil Soup with Lamb Meatballs
recipeIngredient: 7 cups hot chicken or vegetable broth
Additional stock if needed for proper consistency
2/3 cup blanched and toasted almonds (coarsely ground)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
2. Add onions, carrots, garlic, and celery; cook on medium heat, stirring frequently, until onions are transparent. Stir in thyme leaves, turmeric, and cumin; cook for 1 minute.
3. Add broth and lentils. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to simmer. Skim the surface.
4. Simmer for 25 to 30 minutes, or until lentils are soft. Add salt and pepper to taste.
5. Puree half of the soup using a food processor fitted with the metal blade or a blender. Stir the pureed portion back into the soup. Adjust seasoning as needed.
6. Prepare the meatballs:
- Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
- Combine all meatball ingredients in a large bowl. Using clean hands, mix the ingredients thoroughly and form them into 1-inch meatballs.
- Place the meatballs on an oiled foil-lined jelly-roll pan. Bake for about 10 to 13 minutes, or until cooked through.
7. In a small bowl, combine yogurt, mint, and lemon zest.
8. Serve hot soup in individual bowls, garnishing with the yogurt mixture.
Yield: Makes 8-10 servings.
